nodes.label: Validation Presets
nodes.rationale: Pre-configured validation rules (none, email, postal_code_prefix, regex) with auto-filled fields, balancing ease-of-use with flexibility. Only the 'regex' type exposes manual fields to admins.

nodes.label: Question Description Field
nodes.rationale: An optional helper-text/description field available on all question types. Stores context like 'We ask for the first three characters of your postal code to get a general sense of where responses are coming from.' Rendered below question text in muted, italic styling.

nodes.label: Validation Config
nodes.rationale: JSON object stored in the question's options JSONB column with fields: type, regex, max_length, normalize_uppercase. Extends existing per-type configuration pattern without schema migration.

nodes.label: Short Answer Respondent UI Flow
nodes.rationale: Renders an <input> with browser-native maxLength for validated questions (textarea for unvalidated). On step advance: normalizes to uppercase, validates against regex, shows inline error if failed, saves via PUT on success. Optional questions skip validation when empty.

nodes.label: options JSONB
nodes.rationale: The JSONB column on the questions table storing per-type configuration. Its polymorphic nature (choices for MC, validation for short_answer, description for all types) avoids schema migrations — any shape is accepted by PostgreSQL JSONB.

nodes.label: Browser Native maxLength
nodes.rationale: Uses HTML <input maxLength={3}> to provide browser-native length enforcement as a first line of defense before programmatic validation.

nodes.label: No DB Migration Required
nodes.rationale: Both features leverage existing JSONB columns rather than adding new schema columns. The question_type enum already includes short_answer, and options JSONB accepts arbitrary shapes. This avoids migration risks and deployment coordination.

nodes.label: Out of Scope (Short Answer Validation)
nodes.rationale: CSV/Excel export, validation presets beyond email and postal code, server-side i18n for error messages (client-side only), and realtime keystroke validation. These are deferred to keep scope focused on the immediate postal code use case.

nodes.label: Polymorphic Options Pattern
nodes.rationale: The options JSONB column stores different shapes per question type: {choices: [...]} for MC/checkbox/dropdown/ranking, {description: '...'} for section_header, {validation: {...}} for short_answer. This polymorphic pattern eliminates the need for separate option tables per type.
